---
title: "Uplift - Stop targeting people who were going to buy anyway."
image: ../../images/cguplift/cguplift.jpeg
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
:root {
  --accent: #D4537E;
  --muted: #8a8a8a;
  --border: #e4e4e4;
  --text: #1a1a1a;
  --bg-light: #fafafa;
}

.uplift-wrap {
  max-width: 860px;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 2rem 1.5rem 5rem;
}

/* Back link */
.back-link {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  color: var(--muted);
  text-decoration: none;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.1em;
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}
.back-link:hover { color: var(--accent); text-decoration: none; }

/* Tag pills */
.tag-row {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.4rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
}
.tag {
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  color: var(--muted);
  background: transparent;
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.7rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

/* Title block */
.project-title {
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.3;
  color: var(--text);
  margin: 0 0 1rem;
}
.project-sub {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #5a5a5a;
  line-height: 1.7;
  max-width: 720px;
  margin: 0 0 2rem;
}

/* Metadata row */
.meta-row {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 2rem;
  padding: 1.25rem 0;
  border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
  margin-bottom: 3rem;
}
.meta-label {
  font-size: 10px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.15em;
  color: var(--muted);
  font-weight: 600;
  display: block;
  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
}
.meta-value {
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: var(--text);
  font-weight: 500;
}
.meta-value a {
  color: var(--accent);
  text-decoration: none;
}
.meta-value a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

/* Section divider */
.section-hr {
  border: none;
  border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
  margin: 3rem 0;
}
.section-label {
  font-size: 11px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.15em;
  color: var(--muted);
  font-weight: 600;
  margin: 0 0 1.75rem;
}

/* Result cards */
.results-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 3rem;
}
.result-card {
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
  text-align: center;
}
.result-task {
  font-size: 10px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.12em;
  color: var(--muted);
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.result-lift {
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--accent);
  line-height: 1;
}
.result-desc {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  color: var(--muted);
  margin-top: 0.35rem;
}

/* Body text */
.body-text {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: #5a5a5a;
  line-height: 1.75;
  margin: 0 0 1.25rem;
}

/* Pink callout */
.callout-pink {
  background: #fdf0f4;
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent);
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--accent);
  line-height: 1.5;
  border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0;
  margin: 1.5rem 0;
}

/* Segment cards */
.segment-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.segment-card {
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 1.1rem 1rem;
}
.segment-card.highlight {
  border-color: var(--accent);
  background: #fdf0f4;
}
.segment-name {
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--text);
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
}
.segment-card.highlight .segment-name {
  color: var(--accent);
}
.segment-desc {
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  color: #5a5a5a;
  line-height: 1.5;
  margin-bottom: 0.65rem;
}
.segment-action {
  display: inline-block;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.1em;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
}
.action-target {
  background: var(--accent);
  color: #fff;
}
.action-skip {
  background: var(--border);
  color: var(--muted);
}
.action-avoid {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  color: #999;
}

/* Models table */
.models-table {
  width: 100%;
  border-collapse: collapse;
}
.models-table th {
  font-size: 10px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.12em;
  color: var(--muted);
  font-weight: 600;
  text-align: left;
  padding: 0.6rem 1rem 0.6rem 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
}
.models-table td {
  padding: 0.75rem 1rem 0.75rem 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
  color: var(--text);
  font-size: 0.875rem;
}
.best-badge {
  display: inline-block;
  font-size: 0.68rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  color: var(--accent);
  border: 1px solid var(--accent);
  padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  margin-left: 0.5rem;
  vertical-align: middle;
}

/* Features list */
.features-grid {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.5rem;
}
.feature-pill {
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  color: var(--text);
  background: var(--bg-light);
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  padding: 0.3rem 0.85rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
}

/* Method pills */
.method-row {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.35rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.method-pill {
  border: 1px solid var(--accent);
  color: var(--accent);
  background: transparent;
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  padding: 0.18rem 0.65rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

/* Reflection cards */
.reflection-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 1rem;
}
.reflection-card {
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 1.25rem;
}
.reflection-title {
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--text);
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
}
.reflection-desc {
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  color: #5a5a5a;
  line-height: 1.6;
}

@media (max-width: 680px) {
  .results-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .segment-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
  .reflection-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .meta-row { gap: 1rem; }
}
</style>

<div class="uplift-wrap">

  <!-- BACK LINK -->
  <a href="../../projects.html" class="back-link">← All Projects</a>

  <!-- TAGS -->
  <div class="tag-row">
    <span class="tag">Causal Inference</span>
    <span class="tag">Marketing Analytics</span>
    <span class="tag">Python</span>
    <span class="tag">XGBoost</span>
    <span class="tag">Polars</span>
    <span class="tag">pyrsm</span>
  </div>

  <!-- TITLE -->
  <h1 class="project-title">Stop targeting people who were going to buy anyway.</h1>
  <p class="project-sub">Uplift modeling identifies the customers who convert <em>because</em> of a marketing action — not despite it, and not regardless of it. Built on a 120k-user randomized experiment from a mobile game, comparing four treatment effect estimators to find the segment worth targeting.</p>

  <!-- METADATA -->
  <div class="meta-row">
    <div>
      <span class="meta-label">Type</span>
      <span class="meta-value">Causal ML · Marketing</span>
    </div>
    <div>
      <span class="meta-label">Dataset</span>
      <span class="meta-value">120k-user RCT</span>
    </div>
    <div>
      <span class="meta-label">Models</span>
      <span class="meta-value">XGBoost · LR · RF · MLP</span>
    </div>
    <div>
      <span class="meta-label">Code</span>
      <span class="meta-value"><a href="https://github.com/rsm-amarkova/uplift_model_cg" target="_blank">GitHub →</a></span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- RESULTS AT A GLANCE -->
  <p class="section-label">Results at a Glance</p>
  <div class="results-grid">
    <div class="result-card">
      <div class="result-task">Optimal Target</div>
      <div class="result-lift">Top 25%</div>
      <div class="result-desc">of users by predicted uplift score</div>
    </div>
    <div class="result-card">
      <div class="result-task">RCT Dataset</div>
      <div class="result-lift">120k</div>
      <div class="result-desc">users across treatment and control</div>
    </div>
    <div class="result-card">
      <div class="result-task">Models Compared</div>
      <div class="result-lift">4</div>
      <div class="result-desc">treatment effect estimators evaluated</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <hr class="section-hr">

  <!-- PROBLEM -->
  <p class="section-label">The Problem with Propensity Targeting</p>

  <p class="body-text">Most marketing models ask: <em>who is most likely to convert?</em> That sounds right — but it's the wrong question. Targeting high-propensity users wastes budget on two groups: people who would have converted anyway (Sure Things), and people who actually respond negatively to outreach (Sleeping Dogs).</p>

  <p class="body-text">Uplift modeling reframes the question as: <em>for whom does the treatment cause conversion?</em> This is a causal inference problem. The goal is to estimate the individual treatment effect — the difference in conversion probability between a user who receives the action and the same user in a counterfactual world where they don't.</p>

  <div class="callout-pink">
    Maximizing conversion rate is not the same as maximizing profit.
  </div>

  <p class="body-text">Because we can never observe both outcomes for the same person, uplift models are trained on randomized control trial data — where treatment assignment is independent of user characteristics — and the target is the estimated difference in outcomes, not the outcome itself.</p>

  <hr class="section-hr">

  <!-- CUSTOMER SEGMENTS -->
  <p class="section-label">The Four Customer Segments</p>

  <div class="segment-grid">
    <div class="segment-card highlight">
      <div class="segment-name">Persuadables</div>
      <p class="segment-desc">Convert if treated, don't if untreated. These are the only users where the treatment creates genuine incremental value.</p>
      <span class="segment-action action-target">Target</span>
    </div>
    <div class="segment-card">
      <div class="segment-name">Sure Things</div>
      <p class="segment-desc">Convert regardless of treatment. Targeting them wastes spend without any lift in outcome.</p>
      <span class="segment-action action-skip">Skip</span>
    </div>
    <div class="segment-card">
      <div class="segment-name">Lost Causes</div>
      <p class="segment-desc">Won't convert regardless. No treatment will change this — budget is better spent elsewhere.</p>
      <span class="segment-action action-skip">Skip</span>
    </div>
    <div class="segment-card">
      <div class="segment-name">Sleeping Dogs</div>
      <p class="segment-desc">Less likely to convert if treated. Outreach actively hurts conversion — these users must be excluded.</p>
      <span class="segment-action action-avoid">Avoid</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <hr class="section-hr">

  <!-- MODELS -->
  <p class="section-label">Models Compared</p>

  <table class="models-table">
    <thead>
      <tr>
        <th>Model</th>
        <th>Approach</th>
        <th>Notes</th>
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      <tr>
        <td>Logistic Regression</td>
        <td>Two-model (T-learner)</td>
        <td>Baseline; interpretable but misses interaction effects</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td>Random Forest</td>
        <td>Two-model (T-learner)</td>
        <td>Captures nonlinearity; prone to overfitting on uplift signal</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td>XGBoost<span class="best-badge">Best</span></td>
        <td>Two-model (T-learner)</td>
        <td>Highest Qini coefficient; best separation of Persuadables</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td>Neural Network (MLP)</td>
        <td>Shared representation</td>
        <td>Competitive but slower to tune; marginal gain over XGBoost</td>
      </tr>
    </tbody>
  </table>

  <hr class="section-hr">

  <!-- KEY FEATURES -->
  <p class="section-label">Key Features Used</p>
  <p class="body-text">Behavioral signals from the mobile game environment, engineered from user activity logs prior to the experiment window.</p>

  <div class="features-grid">
    <span class="feature-pill">Levels reached</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Game days played</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Ads clicked</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Messages sent</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Friend requests</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Badges earned</span>
    <span class="feature-pill">Device type</span>
  </div>

  <hr class="section-hr">

  <!-- WHAT I'D DO DIFFERENTLY -->
  <p class="section-label">What I'd Do Differently</p>
  <div class="reflection-grid">
    <div class="reflection-card">
      <div class="reflection-title">Add Cost Constraints</div>
      <p class="reflection-desc">The current model optimizes for uplift score alone. A production version should incorporate send cost and expected revenue per conversion to solve for the optimal targeting budget, not just the optimal segment boundary.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="reflection-card">
      <div class="reflection-title">Try the X-Learner</div>
      <p class="reflection-desc">The T-learner treats treatment and control models independently. The X-learner cross-fits the residuals, which tends to reduce variance when treatment and control sample sizes are unequal — common in real campaigns.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="reflection-card">
      <div class="reflection-title">Calibrate Probabilities</div>
      <p class="reflection-desc">Tree-based models like XGBoost output well-ranked but poorly calibrated probabilities. Applying Platt scaling or isotonic regression before taking the difference would produce uplift scores that are meaningful in absolute terms, not just rank order.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
```
